title: Self-Worth Maintenance Checklist
description: An ongoing maintenance checklist to help you sustain the progress you've made in building healthier self-esteem. Use this regularly to stay aware of old patterns and reinforce new ones.
instructions: Review this checklist weekly. Check off the strategies you used this week. Pay attention to any unchecked items — they may highlight areas where old patterns are creeping back in. Consistency matters more than perfection.

items.label: I checked my thoughts for cognitive distortions (all-or-nothing thinking, labeling, etc.)
I used a thought record when a self-critical thought felt strong or sticky
I noticed and challenged a core belief if it was activated (e.g., 'I'm not good enough')
I did something despite fear of judgment or failure
I dropped a safety behavior (e.g., over-apologizing, seeking excessive reassurance, staying silent)
I tried something new or slightly outside my comfort zone
I added to my positive data log (evidence that contradicts negative core beliefs)
I did an activity aligned with my personal values
I acknowledged a personal strength or quality (without dismissing it)
I used compassionate self-talk when I made a mistake or felt inadequate
I practiced a self-compassion exercise (meditation, letter, break, etc.)
I wrote in my compassion journal or reflected on what I'm learning about myself
I reached out to a supportive person when I needed connection
I set a healthy boundary (said no, limited exposure to critical people, etc.)
I reviewed my progress and acknowledged how far I've come

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. Self-esteem work is an ongoing process — setbacks are normal and do not erase your progress. If you notice old patterns returning strongly, revisit earlier lessons or speak with a mental health professional. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
